title: Fix the Cascading Return Type Change
description: A function's return type changed from dict to a dataclass, breaking all dict-style access in consumers and tests.
pr_diff:
PR Diff:
  - def fetch_config() -> dict:
  -     return {"host": "db.local", "port": 5432}
  + @dataclass
  + class Config:
  +     host: str
  +     port: int
  + def fetch_config() -> Config:
  +     return Config(host="db.local", port=5432)
initial_ci_output:
[FAILED] mypy
error: Item "Config" of ... has no attribute "__getitem__" [index-error] (x4)

[FAILED] unit_tests
TypeError: 'Config' object is not subscriptable

[FAILED] integration
KeyError: config['host'] no longer valid

[FAILED] full_suite
Multiple failures from dict→dataclass migration

files.src/server.py:
from src.config_loader import fetch_config


def start_server():
    config = fetch_config()
    print(f"Listening on {config['host']}:{config['port']}")
    return config
files.tests/test_config.py:
from src.config_loader import fetch_config


def test_config_host():
    config = fetch_config()
    assert config["host"] == "db.local"


def test_config_port():
    config = fetch_config()
    assert config["port"] == 5432
files.tests/test_integration.py:
from src.config_loader import fetch_config


def test_full_config():
    config = fetch_config()
    assert config["host"] == "db.local" and config["port"] == 5432

mypy.failure_output: mypy: 'Config' object is not subscriptable [index-error] (x4 across database.py, server.py, tests)

unit_tests.failure_output: TypeError: 'Config' object is not subscriptable in test_config_host and test_config_port

integration.failure_output: KeyError: 'Config' object does not support bracket access in integration test

full_suite.failure_output: full suite still has failures from dict→dataclass migration in 5 files
full_suite.success_output: full suite green — all bracket access converted to attribute access
